title: The One With The Cooking Class

lines.text: Central Perk, everyone is there except Monica as Ross enters carrying a huge stack of newspapers.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey you guys I got some bad news. (He sets the stack of papers down on the table.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well that's no way to sell newspapers. Why don't you try, "Extra! Extra! Read all about it!"

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: No, Monica's restaurant got a horrible review in the Post. (They all gasp.) I didn't want her to see it, so I ran around the neighborhood and bought all the copies I could find. (He hands the paper to Phoebe and they all read it.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Man, this is bad! And I've had my share of bad reviews. I still remember my first good one though. (Quoting) "Everything else in this production of Our Town was simply terrible. Joey Tribbiani was abysmal."

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (entering) Hey!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (seeing the stack of newspapers) Oh my God! Look at all the newspapers! It must be a good review! Is it great?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Umm...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: (reading) Oh dear God!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: But the good news is, no one in a two-block radius will ever know.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: What about the rest of Manhattan?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah, they all know.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh my God, this is horrible!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'm so sorry.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm so humiliated!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah but y'know what they say Mon, "There's no such thing as bad press."

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You don't think that umm, (reading) "The chef's Mahi Mahi was awful awful," is bad press?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I didn't write it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Is he right? Am I really-Am I awful?

lines.speaker: All
lines.text: No!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah! Yeah Monica! You listen to me, okay? And I'm not just saying this because I'm your friend, I'm sayin' it 'cause it's the truth. You're food is abysmal!
Opening Credits
Ross and Rachel's, it's late at night, Rachel is sitting on the couch in the dark wide-awake as Ross walks to the bathroom.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Ross!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (startling him) What?! What?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I am freaking out!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Are ya?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: My due date is in one week!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What are you doing up?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: That is seven days!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay look, I had a lot of water before I went to bed. Can we do this after...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (interrupting him) No-no-no-no-no Ross! Please, come on we do not have any of the big stuff we need! We do not a changing table! We do not have a crib! We do not have a diaper service!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: It's funny you should mention diapers.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm serious.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay look, there's nothing to worry about. We have plenty of time. There's a great baby furniture store on west 10th. Tomorrow, we will go there and we will get you everything that you need. Okay?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Okay. Thank you. That's great. Thank you. Wait-wait! Where on west 10th? Because there's this really cute shoe store that has like this little...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (interrupting her) Okay. Okay. If uh, if you're gonna do this, then I'm gonna do that. (Points to the bathroom.) So... (Starts for the bathroom.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (stopping him) Oh, wait Ross! I'm sorry, one more thing!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (annoyed) Yeah!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Umm, our situation. Y'know umm, what we mean to each other. And I mean we-we're having this baby together, and we live together. Isn't that, isn't that weird?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (stunned) (thinks) Well uh...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm just kidding! You can go pee! (He does so in a hurry.)
[Scene: Monica and Chandler's, Monica is cooking as Chandler looks on.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: (entering) Hey uh Monica, I can't remember. Did we say we were gonna meet here or at the movies?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: We said at the movies, but...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Okay, I'll see you there. (Starts to leave)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey! (He returns) Now that you're here...

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Sure, I can hang out 'til I have to meet ya. (To Chandler) What uh-How come you're not going?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I have a job interview I have to get ready for.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I thought you already have a job.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: And people say you don't pay attention. No, this is a much better job. It's vice-president of a company that does data reconfiguration and statistical factoring for other companies.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Wow! How do you know how to do that?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That's what I do now.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hey Joey, come taste this.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What is it?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Remember that guy that gave me a bad review? Well... (Feeds him a spoonful of what she's cooking.) I'm getting my revenge!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: You cooked him?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: No. He teaches a course on food criticism at the New School, so before we go to the movies I wanna go by there and make him try my bouillabaisse again. Oh, I cannot wait to read the front page of the Post tomorrow! "Restaurant reviewer admits: I was wrong about Monica."

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: The front page? You really do live in your own little world, don't ya?
The Baby Furniture Store, Ross and Rachel are checking out.

lines.speaker: Cashier
lines.text: Do you uh, want these things delivered Mr. and Mrs. Geller?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No-no-no! No, no, no, we're not married.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We are having a baby together, but we're not involved. (The cashier, a very beautiful woman, looks confused) I mean, uh we-we were seeing each other a while ago, but then we were just friends. And then there was one drunken night. (Rachel looks at him angrily) Or, yes stranger, we'd like this delivered please.

lines.speaker: Cashier
lines.text: Why don't you fill out this address card. (Hands him one.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh, okay.

lines.speaker: Cashier
lines.text: I notice you picked out a lot of our dinosaur items.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah! Actually, that's one of the reasons why we're not a couple.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I chose those, I'm a paleontologist.

lines.speaker: Cashier
lines.text: Really?! That is so cool!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh. Oh yeah, don't get to worked up over it. I mean it-it sounds like he's a doctor, but he's not.

lines.speaker: Cashier
lines.text: Oh no-no, I'm fascinated by paleontology. Have you read the new Walter Alvarez book?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Yeah! I-I teach it in my class.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh my God! I'm standing at a cash register, holding a credit card, and I'm bored.

lines.speaker: Cashier
lines.text: (looking at the completed address card) Oh, I love your neighborhood. There's a great gym right around the corner from your building.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: That's my gym.

lines.speaker: Cashier
lines.text: I can tell you work out. (Ross is please and Rachel looks at him confused.) A paleontologist who works out, you're like Indiana Jones. (Rachel has a disbelieving look on her face.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I am like Indiana Jones.
Central Perk, Phoebe is there as Rachel enters.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi Pheebs!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey! Oh, how did baby shopping go?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh, it was great! We got everything that we needed! Oh and Ross, almost got something that wasn't on the list. A whore.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Well, we were paying for our stuff and this saleswoman just started flirting with him.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well did she know you two weren't married?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh my God! Well the idea of a woman flirting with a-with a single man, we-we must alert the church elders!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: You don't understand! You didn't see how brazen she was.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Sounds like you're a little jealous.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! I'm not! I-I-I just think it's wrong! It's-it's that I'm-Here I am about to pop and he's out picking up some shop girl at Sluts 'R' Us!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Is that a real place? (Rachel's stunned) Are they hiring?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (entering) Hey Phoebe! (To Rachel) Fatty!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Hey Chandler, why so fancy?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well, I got a job interview. It's kinda a big deal too. Its a lot more money and I'd be doing data reconfiguration and statistical factoring.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Wait, I think I know someone who does that.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Me! I do that. So... Seriously, do I look okay? I'm little nervous.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah! You really-You look great.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yeah, just don't get your hopes up.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Why not?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well, the interview...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What about it?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Y'know! You don't make a very good first impression.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (shocked) What?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh you don't know.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Are you serious?!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Yes, when I first met you, you were like, "Blah, blah, blah." I was like, shhh!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: What is it that I do?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Well it's just like you're trying too hard. Always making jokes, y'know, you just-You come off a little needy.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (To Rachel) Did you like me when we first met?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Chandler, I'm not gonna lie to ya, but I am gonna run away from you. (Gets up and hurries out.)
The New School, Monica, carrying her dish, and Joey are confronting the food critic.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Hi! Umm, I'm Monica Geller, I'm the chef at Alessandro's.

lines.speaker: The Food Critic
lines.text: Still?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I think the things that you said about me are really unfair, and I would like for you to give my bouillabaisse another chance.

lines.speaker: The Food Critic
lines.text: I don't see any reason why I would do that to myself again.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Either eat it, or be in it.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Spoon? (Hands him one and he tastes it.) So, what do you think?

lines.speaker: The Food Critic
lines.text: I'm torn, between my integrity and my desire to avoid a beating. But I must be honest, your soap is abysmal. (Throws down the spoon and walks out.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Thata girl! Huh? We should get out of here; there's a new class comin' in.
They start to leave.

lines.speaker: The Cooking Teacher
lines.text: Welcome to introduction to cooking. Now, before we start, can anyone tell me the difference between a hollandaise sauce and a bearnaise sauce? (No one can.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I can.

lines.speaker: The Cooking Teacher
lines.text: Okay, go ahead.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Well umm, they both have a egg yolk and butter base, but a bearnaise has shallots, shirvel, and most importantly tarragon.

lines.speaker: The Cooking Teacher
lines.text: That's very good, what's your name?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Monica.

lines.speaker: The Cooking Teacher
lines.text: Monica, you go to the head of the class.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay. (Does so.)
Ross and Rachel's, Ross and Rachel are unpacking and setting up their new purchases.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: All this stuff takes up a lot of room. Hey how uh, how serious are you about keeping Ben in your life?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: My son? Pretty serious. (There's a knock on the door and Ross answers it.) Oh hey Katie! (The cashier from before) What uh, what are you doing here?

lines.speaker: Katie
lines.text: Well, the delivery went out to you and I realized they forgot this. (A blanket.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Ah, must've been fairly obvious since it was the only thing left in your store.

lines.speaker: Katie
lines.text: Listen, to be honest, home deliveries are really a part of my job description.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh. (Reaches into his pocket for some money as Rachel enters the living room and watches holding two stuffed dinosaurs.)

lines.speaker: Katie
lines.text: Oh uh...I actually came here to ask you out.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh! Wow! Uh, yeah! That sounds great. I'm just gonna put this (The money) back in my pocket, pretend that didn't happen. Uh yeah, actually I'm free now. Do you wanna grab some coffee or...

lines.speaker: Katie
lines.text: Sure!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Horny bitch. (They both look at her, pretending that the dinosaurs she's holding are arguing.) No! You're a horny bitch! Noooo! You're the horny bitch! No! You're a horny bitch!
Commercial Break
Ross and Rachel's, continued from before.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So you guys go, have a really good time.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (To Katie) Yeah, I'm just gonna grab my coat. And uh, and my whip. (Katie looks worried.) Y'know because of the Indiana Jones? (Katie laughs) Not-not because I'm-I'm into S&M. (Katie's worried again.) I'm not-I'm not into anything weird. Y'know? Just-just normal sex. (Katie is uncomfortable.) So, I'm gonna grab my coat. (Does so, leaving Katie and Rachel alone.)

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: So, you had a good day huh? Big commission; picked up a daddy.

lines.speaker: Katie
lines.text: Are you okay with this?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh yeah! Yeah please, you guys have fun.

lines.speaker: Katie
lines.text: Okay. It was nice to see you.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh and it was great to see you too. And you look fantastic, although you missed a button.

lines.speaker: Katie
lines.text: Oh umm, actually I umm...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh okay, I see what you're doing there.
Monica and Chandler's, Chandler and Phoebe are entering.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I can't even believe this! I really come off that badly?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Oh! It's okay, you calm down after a while and then people can see how really sweet and wonderful you really are.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh good. Good, because I'm sure this interview is gonna last a couple of weeks.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, don't freak out! Okay? I-I will help you. How long before you have to leave?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: An hour.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I can't help you.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Phoebe!

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: All right, all right, we'll just do our best. Okay? So let's say I'm the interviewer and I'm meeting you for the first time. Okay. "Hi! Come on in, I'm uh, Regina Philange."

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Chandler Bing.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Bing, what an unusual name.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Well you should meet my uncle, Bada. (Pause) I'll let myself out.
The Cooking Class, Joey is trying to cook as the teacher walks over to him.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hi.

lines.speaker: The Cooking Teacher
lines.text: Your Fettuccini Alfredo looks a little dry, did you use all your cheese?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: When you say used, do you mean eat as a pre-cooking snack?

lines.speaker: The Cooking Teacher
lines.text: And the cream?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Cheese makes me thirsty.

lines.speaker: The Cooking Teacher
lines.text: Okay. Let's move on.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: All right.

lines.speaker: The Cooking Teacher
lines.text: (To Monica) Oh! Something smells good over at Monica's station! (She tries Monica's fettuccini.) Oh my God! This is absolutely amazing! You've never made this before?

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh no! I don't know anything about cooking. I had to ask someone what it's called when the, when the water makes those little bubbles.

lines.speaker: The Cooking Teacher
lines.text: Well, hats off to the chef.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I-I-I'm sorry, your-your mouth was full, I didn't hear what you said. Umm, hats off to who now?

lines.speaker: The Cooking Teacher
lines.text: The chef!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: That's right.
Monica and Chandler's, Chandler and Phoebe are still working on his interviewing techniques.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: ...I think you'll find if I come to work here, I don't micro-manage. I don't shy away from delegating.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Um-hmm, that's good to know. But let's stop focusing on what you don't do, and start focusing on what you do do.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (suppressing a smile) What I do do...is manage to uh, create an atmosphere of support for the people working with me.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: I see. Nice sidestep on the do do thing by the way.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Hardest thing I've ever done in my life.

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: You gotta go!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh! (Stands up.)

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Okay, don't worry. You're ready.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Really?

lines.speaker: Phoebe
lines.text: Absolutely! Just fight all your natural instincts and you'll be great.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Okay.
The Cooking Class, everyone has finished baking a batch of cookies and the teacher is going around tasting them.

lines.speaker: The Cooking Teacher
lines.text: Ah Monica, my star student.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Y'know, you called me that before so I-I took the liberty of fashioning a star out of aluminum foil. Now, no pressure, you like my cookies, you give me the star. (Hands it to her.)

lines.speaker: The Cooking Teacher
lines.text: (tasting the cookie and with her mouthful) Oh, yum-yum-yum. (Hands the star back.)

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Wow! A star! (The class glares at her.) I know you all hate me and-and I'm sorry, but I don't care.
The teacher goes to Joey's station.

lines.speaker: The Cooking Teacher
lines.text: Okay Joey, you're up next. (Tries one of his cookies.) This are good! This is amazing! You get an A!

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: I can an A? In-in school? (To Joey) Hey, I'm a dork.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Joey! I'm so proud of you!

lines.speaker: The Cooking Teacher
lines.text: I think you should give him your star.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Excuse me? He doesn't even know what he's doing!

lines.speaker: The Cooking Teacher
lines.text: We're all beginners here. Nobody knows what they're doing.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I do! I'm a professional chef! (The class gasps.) Oh relax! It's not a courtroom drama!

lines.speaker: The Cooking Teacher
lines.text: If you're a professional chef, what are you doing taking Introduction to Cooking?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: I'm-I'm sorry, it's just that umm... Well I-I cook at this restaurant, Alessandro's, and umm I just got a really bad review...

lines.speaker: The Cooking Teacher
lines.text: Oh Alessandro's! I love that place!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: You do?

lines.speaker: The Cooking Teacher
lines.text: Oh yes! You're an excellent chef! As a person you're a little...

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, I'm totally crazy, but you-you like the food?

lines.speaker: The Cooking Teacher
lines.text: Very much.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay then, I don't stink. I'm a good chef. Okay. (Starts to leave.)

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Whoa-whoa-whoa-whoa! I don't want to go. I'm having fun.

lines.speaker: The Cooking Teacher
lines.text: Well actually, did either of you pay for this class?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Hey-hey-hey, if my friend says it's time to go, it's time to go. (Starts to leave, but comes back for his cookies.)
An Office Building, Chandler is on his interview.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: ...also I was the point person on my company's transition from the KL-5 to GR-6 system.

lines.speaker: The Interviewer
lines.text: You must've had your hands full.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: That I did. That I did.

lines.speaker: The Interviewer
lines.text: So let's talk a little bit about your duties.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (nervous) My duties? (Trying not to crack a joke) All right.

lines.speaker: The Interviewer
lines.text: Now you'll be heading a whole division, so you'll have a lot of duties.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (trying not to laugh) I see.

lines.speaker: The Interviewer
lines.text: But there'll be perhaps 30 people under you so you can dump a certain amount on them.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (really try not to laugh) Good to know.

lines.speaker: The Interviewer
lines.text: We can go into detail...

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: No don't I beg of you!

lines.speaker: The Interviewer
lines.text: All right then, we'll have a definite answer for you on Monday, but I think I can say with some confidence, you'll fit in well here.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: (relieved) Really?!

lines.speaker: The Interviewer
lines.text: Absolutely. (They walk to the door.) You can relax; you did great.

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Yeah I gotta say thank you, I was really nervous. Y'know I've been told I come on to strong, make to many jokes, and then it was really hard to sidestep that duty thing. (The interviewer doesn't understand) Duties. (Still doesn't.) Duties! (Still doesn't.) Poo. (Still doesn't.)

lines.speaker: The Interviewer
lines.text: Poo?

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: Oh my God this doesn't count! Okay? The interview was over, that was the real Chandler Bing in there, this is just some crazy guy out in the hall! Call security! There's a crazy guy out in the hall!

lines.speaker: The Interviewer
lines.text: Poo?!

lines.speaker: Chandler
lines.text: I'll look forward to your call. (Walks away.)
Ross and Rachel's, Ross is returning from his date.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Hey.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Hi! You're back from your date!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: How are you?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I'm fine, but that's not important. What's important is how was she?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uhh, it was fun. We, we just had coffee.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh uh-huh, uh-huh, coffee, a little rub-rub-rub under the table.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: What's uh, what's going on? Do you not, do you not like Katie?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! No, she's-She was nice. I mean, she's a little slutty, but who isn't?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I liked her.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Of course you did Ross, you would date a gorilla if it called you Indiana Jones!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Did you get like a fresh batch of pregnancy hormones today?!

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No! It's just that, Kate bothered me.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Why? What was wrong with her?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: There was nothing wrong with her! All right? She was perfectly lovely!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay, so what's the matter?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: I don't want you to date her!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (laughs) Why? What, what are you jealous?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yes! And not because I want you to go out with me, but because I don't want you to go out with anybody! Okay? I know it's a terrible thing to even think this, and it's completely inappropriate, but I want you to be at my constant beck and call 24 hours a day! I'm very sorry, but that is just the way that I feel.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Okay.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: What?!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: I won't date. I'll uh, I'll be here, with you, all the time.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Really? But I'm being so unreasonable.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: True, but you're allowed to be unreasonable. You're having our baby.
Pause.

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (starting to cry) Oh Ross, thank you. Thank you. (They hug.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Do you feel better?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No, not really. You're pressing the baby into my bladder and now I have to pee. Sorry. (She gets up and starts for the bathroom.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Uh Rach?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah. (Stops and starts doing the I-have-to-go-to-the-bathroom dance.)

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Just one thing umm...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Uh-huh.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: We live together. You're having our baby. I'm not gonna see anybody else. Are you-are you sure you don't want something more?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (pause) Wow! I don't know, maybe. I'm...

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Oh-oh, Rach! I was just messin' around! (She's stunned) Like you did last night when I had to pee?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: (laughs) I knew that! I knew that! I was just messin' with you too!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: (pause) Okay. Okay. Because for a minute you said you...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Oh no-no-no-no, no!

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: ...that you actually...

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: No that's just-(Laughs)-That's just 'cause I'm such a good messer!
They both laugh. Ross sits down, but Rachel doesn't move and is lost in thought.

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: Rach?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Yeah?

lines.speaker: Ross
lines.text: The bathroom?

lines.speaker: Rachel
lines.text: Right! (Heads for the bathroom.)
Closing Credits
The New School, Joey and Monica are walking down a hallway.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Well I had a great time! Learned how to bake, ate great food, that's the first A I've gotten since seventh grade, and I didn't have to sleep with the teacher this time.

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Oh, look! Acting for Beginners! Want to feel good about yourself?

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: What the hell!

lines.speaker: Monica
lines.text: Okay.
They enter.

lines.speaker: The Acting Teacher
lines.text: All right, let's start with some basics. Can anybody tell me what the difference between upstage and downstage is?
No one can and Monica looks at Joey expectantly.

lines.speaker: Joey
lines.text: Yeah, this was a stupid idea. (Exits.)
End
